title: C Comments
content:
### Comments in C

Comments are used to explain code and are ignored by the compiler.

### Single-Line Comments

Single-line comments start with `//`. Everything after `//` on that line is ignored.

### Multi-Line Comments

Multi-line comments start with `/*` and end with `*/`. They can span multiple lines.

---

### Comment Usage Guidelines

| Type | Syntax | Use Case |
|------|--------|----------|
| Single-line | `// text` | Short explanations, inline notes |
| Multi-line | `/* text */` | Block descriptions, disabling code |
| Documentation | `/** ... */` | Function/API documentation (Doxygen) |

---

> **Key Point:** Comments cannot be nested. A `/*` inside another `/* */` block will cause an error.

quiz.question: Which symbol starts a single-line comment in C?
